title: ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. villosa
intro: Ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. villosa, commonly known as the red helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are believed to provide relief from various health issues. The health benefits associated with the red helleborine include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
